blockingIssues.description: tests/pytest.ini narrows `testpaths` to a curated subset of directories, so `.venv-mission/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v` stops collecting real backend suites such as `tests/test_tushare_unified/` and `tests/tradingagents/` instead of repairing repository-wide backend collection.

tests/conftest.py ignores all top-level `tests/test*.py` files, which still hides real automated backend coverage such as `tests/test_tradingagents_runtime_settings.py` and leaves repo-wide collection semantics untrustworthy.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit exception for baseline-repair features so workers may investigate and fix a failing initial manifest validator instead of returning immediately on the first baseline failure.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `repair-backend-test-baseline` handoff records a deviation from worker-base step `1.3 Baseline Validation` because the feature's purpose was to repair the failing manifest test command itself.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: State that baseline-repair work must preserve or expand actual automated backend pytest coverage; workers must not make `pytest tests/ -v` pass by narrowing collection or ignore hooks unless suites are explicitly reclassified with documented rationale.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both reviewed baseline-repair features made the manifest test command green partly by excluding real backend suites (`tests/test_tushare_unified/*`, `tests/tradingagents/*`, then top-level `tests/test*.py`) rather than restoring trustworthy repo-wide collection semantics.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the current automated-vs-manual root-level test classification in shared library state right now.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: the current classification is part of the blocking problem under review, so codifying it in shared factual docs now would risk legitimizing broken baseline behavior.
